Title -  INC100029: P1 Website Down

Impact Statement - All users globally are facing issues in navigating through the ONline Boutique Website as they're encountering errors and the website is down. 

Latest update: We are aware of the issue and working on mitigating it. Further updates will be shared as we make progress
@bob  what % of customers do you think are able to access the website?
Or is it down completely for everyone
right now its completely down
ok
Its a global issue. There is no user activity
@hamed  do you think we should scale up our hardware resources? are we hosted on the cloud or are we using onprem hardware
This looks like a data center issue
I have a contact at our data center. may be we should check there
I can contact them
Please contact them and raise a P1 ticket with them
Ask them to check the network switch first
Sure will do
@bez looks like the issue is stemming from the Data center
Keep me updated
Just got in contact with my contact who works at the data centre
:star: Manage actions and timings
Apparently there's a issue with the AC unit's which is causing the servers in that data centres to overheat and shut down.
She has been told it should be an easy fix and they're restarting the servers while addressing the AC units
Dont mean to be interupt this conversation but how does the data centre affect us?
The data centre is where physical servers are hosted it provides CPU and memory to our k8s clusters.
If the server at the data center goes down the node in our infrastructure will stop running causing microservices like the frontend payments etc to stop functioning
I think we are ok now as my contact has just told me that they have successfully restarted the server.
@tanya  can you check if they have a failover option?
Your right i can also see that the website is back up and running again

What's their Disaster recovery or BCP plan?
@shay  no
The server must be down again because the platform isn't working for me anymore
Yes we do have a failover plan if AC fix is going to take longer than expected
Im going to be honest and say we wait for the ac unit to be fixed
I reckon they shouldn't take much longer to address the ac issue
we need to explore our options to perform a disaster recovery
@Tanya @chandan.nagaraja-thomsonreuters.com we could swap traffic to another data centre
No Hamed that is a huge risk which could take even longer than needed
@chandan.nagaraja-thomsonreuters.com every Quarter we plan scheduled data centre migrations mainly for disaster recovery purposes
We could move traffic to another Data centre in N.America
It could take too long Hamed a previous RTO in Q2 took over 2 hours
How long do we need to do that?
and what are the risks involved?
Your right Tanya but in Q3 the migration only took 50 minutes still not great but better than 2 hours
Yes but anything could easily go wrong and make things worse for our customer base using the N.America servers
@tanya  hold your thoughts
The safer option would be to wait for the ac units to be fixed
We could wait or we can take action now it hasn't been confirmed that the ac units will even get fixed soon

@tanya have the Data center folks given an ETA as to how long we wait for a complete resolution?

Is this going to be fixed soon ?
Got an update from them
ACs are beyond fix

ok Never mind

Title -  INC100029: P1 Website Down

Impact Statement - All users globally were facing issues in navigating through the ONline Boutique Website as they were encountering errors and the website was down. 

Latest update: The issue is mitigated and the website is accessible. Customers are able to place orders successfully.
